title: Site Publisher
description: We publish a local folder of HTML files to an S3 static site with the real AWS SDK. By the end you have a public site URL, a bucket that is created idempotently, and a publisher that re-uploads only the files whose content changed.
setup_intro: The app talks to a real AWS account, so it needs credentials. Copy the env template and fill it in:
env_region: <0>AWS_REGION</0> — the region the bucket lives in, such as <1>us-east-1</1>
env_access_key: <0>AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID</0> — access key of an IAM user with S3 permissions
env_bucket: <0>SITE_BUCKET</0> — a globally unique bucket name for the site
warning_real_aws: This app creates real resources in your AWS account, and the bucket it creates is public. Storage for a site this small costs fractions of a cent, but you pay for whatever traffic the site serves.
warning_cleanup: When you are done, delete the bucket and the local ledger: <0>aws s3 rb s3://$SITE_BUCKET --force</0>, then <1>rm -rf ./.state</1>.
run_intro: Start the app. It converges once and exits:
run_expected: On the first run every file uploads and the site URL prints:
code_expected_output:
publishing 3 files from ./site
uploaded about.html
uploaded index.html
uploaded styles.css
site available at http://<SITE_BUCKET>.s3-website-<AWS_REGION>.amazonaws.com
run_outro: Open the printed URL in a browser and S3 serves the index page. The rest of this walkthrough builds that result one portion at a time.
failing_fast_intro: Publishing half a site with missing credentials is worse than not starting, so the app reads its environment at module load and refuses to run without it:
failing_fast_outro: Each variable resolves once, at startup. A missing value throws with the exact fix before any AWS call happens.
manifest_shape_intro: Diff-aware uploads need a picture of what the site looks like right now. A manifest entry records each file's path, content hash, and size:
manifest_shape_outro: The manifest is plain data. Nothing about it is AWS-specific, which keeps it easy to test on its own.
walk_intro: Building the manifest is a recursive walk over the site folder:
walk_outro: Entries are sorted by path, so the manifest stays stable across runs and operating systems.
hashing_intro: Each file is fingerprinted with an md5 hash of its content:
hashing_outro: The hash is what makes the publisher diff-aware. Identical content produces an identical hash, so unchanged files can be skipped later.
manifest_store_intro: The manifest becomes reactive state through <0>createStore</0>:
manifest_store_outro: The store wraps the plain object in fine-grained reactivity, so components track exactly the entries they read. <0>unwrap</0> hands back the raw object whenever plain data is needed.
app_store_intro: The root component builds the store once and announces how many files it is publishing:
app_store_outro: Components run once in CReact, so this body is setup code. The <0>createEffect</0> re-runs only when the file list changes.
context_intro: Every AWS component needs the same region and the same S3 client. A context carries that connection down the tree:
context_outro: The default value is <0>null</0>, which lets consumers detect a missing provider instead of silently using a broken client.
provider_intro: The provider creates one real <0>S3Client</0>, registers its teardown, and wraps its subtree:
provider_outro: <0>onCleanup</0> destroys the client when the provider unmounts, and the <1>children</1> helper resolves the subtree once before it renders.
use_aws_intro: Consumers reach the connection through a small hook that fails loudly:
use_aws_outro: Calling <0>useAws</0> outside the provider throws at render time, instead of surfacing later as a confusing SDK error.
defaults_intro: The bucket component fills in default documents with <0>mergeProps</0> and separates its own props from the children with <1>splitProps</1>:
defaults_outro: Callers only have to pass a name. <0>splitProps</0> leaves <1>rest.children</1> untouched for the return value at the end of the component.
ensure_bucket_intro: The bucket itself is a durable resource declared with <0>useAsyncOutput</0>. The handler starts by creating the bucket and treating already-owned as success:
ensure_bucket_outro: Catching <0>BucketAlreadyOwnedByYou</0> makes the handler idempotent. Running it twice converges to the same bucket instead of failing.
public_access_intro: A fresh bucket blocks all public access, so the handler unblocks it and attaches a public-read policy:
public_access_outro: Both calls are safe to repeat. They describe the state the bucket should be in, not a one-time action.
policy_intro: The policy itself is a plain S3 policy document scoped to objects in this bucket:
policy_outro: Only <0>s3:GetObject</0> is granted. Visitors can read pages, and nobody can list or write.
website_config_intro: With access sorted, the handler enables static website hosting and records its outputs:
website_config_outro: <0>setOutputs</0> persists the bucket name and site URL to the ledger and exposes them as reactive accessors like <1>bucket.url()</1>.
bucket_location_intro: One S3 quirk hides in a helper. <0>us-east-1</0> is the only region that rejects an explicit location constraint:
bucket_location_outro: Every other region requires the constraint, so the helper returns the right shape for both cases.
site_url_intro: Finally an effect watches the URL output and prints it once the bucket is ready:
site_url_outro: The effect tracks <0>bucket.url()</0>, so it fires exactly when the output lands. Rendering <1>rest.children</1> nests the uploads inside the bucket, and children deploy after their parent.
object_props_intro: Each file in the manifest becomes a <0>SiteObject</0>. Its props accept plain values or accessors, normalized with <1>access</1> and <2>unwrap</2>:
object_props_outro: Passing the props as a getter function makes them reactive. When an entry's hash changes, the handler re-runs for just that file.
changed_check_intro: The handler asks the ledger whether this exact content is already published, using the <0>prev</0> argument of <1>setOutputs</1>:
changed_check_outro: <0>prev</0> holds the last persisted outputs, even across restarts. If the stored hash matches the manifest hash, the handler returns without touching S3.
upload_intro: Only changed files reach the upload path:
upload_outro: After the put succeeds, <0>setOutputs</0> stores the new hash. That write is what makes the next run skip this file.
content_type_intro: Browsers need the right <0>Content-Type</0> header, so a small lookup maps extensions to MIME types:
content_type_outro: Anything unknown falls back to <0>application/octet-stream</0>, which downloads instead of rendering.
tree_intro: The root component composes everything — provider outside, bucket inside it, one object per manifest entry:
tree_outro: <0>For</0> keys each child by file path, so adding a file mounts one new resource and removing one disposes it. The bucket deploys before any object, because parents deploy first.
entry_intro: The entry point wires the app to a file-backed ledger and names the stack:
entry_outro: State lands in <0>./.state</0>. Delete that folder and the next run re-uploads everything, because the ledger no longer remembers any hashes.
checkpoint_second_run: Run <0>npm start</0> a second time without touching anything. No <1>uploaded</1> lines appear, because every stored hash matches the manifest.
checkpoint_edit: Now edit <0>site/about.html</0> and run again. Only that one file re-uploads. That is the whole publisher — the tree declares what should exist, and the runtime converges the difference.
recap_provider: A context provider shares one real S3 client with every AWS component in the tree.
recap_idempotent: The bucket handler is idempotent — create, unblock, attach policy, enable hosting are all safe to repeat.
recap_manifest: An md5 manifest in a store turns each file into one durable resource.
recap_prev: The <0>prev</0> argument of <1>setOutputs</1> turns the ledger into a change detector.
recap_ordering: Parents deploy before children, so the bucket exists before any upload starts.
challenge: Add a page. Create <0>site/contact.html</0>, link it from <1>index.html</1>, and run the publisher again. Both changed files upload, nothing else does, and the new page is live at the site URL.
